summary: Iteration 149: OAuth Feature (Google + Microsoft) - All tests PASSED. Backend: 14/14 tests passed. Frontend: All UI elements verified. OAuth buttons, separator, AuthCallback route, and OAuth-only user error messages all working correctly.

backend_api_tests.test_microsoft_login_returns_authorizati: PASS - Returns valid authorization_url with client_id, redirect_uri, scopes
backend_api_tests.test_microsoft_login_url_contains_correc: PASS - URL contains openid, email, profile scopes
backend_api_tests.test_user_without_oauth_has_no_provider_: PASS - No google_id/microsoft_id on email users

frontend_tests.signin_page_google_button: PASS - data-testid='oauth-google-btn' present, text='Continuer avec Google'
frontend_tests.signin_page_microsoft_button: PASS - data-testid='oauth-microsoft-btn' present, text='Continuer avec Microsoft'
frontend_tests.signin_page_separator: PASS - 'OU CONTINUER AVEC EMAIL' separator visible
frontend_tests.signin_page_form_elements: PASS - Email, password inputs and submit button present
frontend_tests.signup_page_separator: PASS - 'OU CONTINUER AVEC EMAIL' separator visible
frontend_tests.auth_callback_route: PASS - /auth/callback route exists, redirects to /signin when no session_id/code
frontend_tests.oauth_buttons_clickable: PASS - Both OAuth buttons are clickable (not disabled)
frontend_tests.email_password_login_flow: PASS - Login with igaal@hotmail.com works, redirects to dashboard
frontend_tests.oauth_only_user_error_display: PASS - Error message 'Ce compte utilise Google pour se connecter. Utilisez le bouton « Continuer avec Google ».' displayed correctly

critical_code_review_comments: oauth_routes.py: _find_or_create_oauth_user correctly handles account linking (same email = link provider, new email = create user)
oauth_routes.py: _build_jwt_response returns is_new_account flag for frontend to show appropriate toast
auth_service.py: login_user correctly detects OAuth-only accounts (password_hash=None) and returns clear error with provider name
OAuthButtons.js: Uses correct Emergent Auth URL for Google, fetches Microsoft authorization_url from backend
AuthCallback.js: Handles both Google (session_id in hash) and Microsoft (code in query params) callbacks
AuthContext.js: loginWithToken function properly stores token and user in localStorage
SignIn.js and SignUp.js: Both include OAuthButtons component with separator
seed_data_creation: Created temporary OAuth-only test user (oauth_only_test_user@test.com) for testing, cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: OAuth feature (Google Emergent Auth + Microsoft Azure AD) fully implemented and tested. Full OAuth flow cannot be tested end-to-end in automated tests since it requires real browser interaction with external providers. Backend endpoints return correct responses, frontend pages show OAuth buttons with correct testids, AuthCallback handles both providers, account linking logic works, and OAuth-only user error messages are clear. Microsoft redirect URI is https://litigation-mgmt.preview.emergentagent.com/auth/callback - user needs to add this in Azure Portal.

google_oauth.behavior: Exchanges Emergent Auth session_id for user data, creates/links account, returns JWT

microsoft_oauth.behavior: Returns authorization_url, then exchanges code for user data, creates/links account, returns JWT

account_linking.behavior: If email exists, provider_id is added to existing user without creating duplicate

oauth_only_login_error.behavior: OAuth-only user attempting password login gets clear error mentioning the provider (Google/Microsoft)
